operating_state: Net sales reached $40.2 billion in Q3 2024, increasing 6.6% driven primarily by the SRS acquisition contributing $2.9 billion, partially offset by negative comparable sales of 1.3% reflecting decreased customer transactions and average ticket. Online sales represented 14.0% of quarterly sales, growing 4.0% year-over-year, with Power, Outdoor Garden, and Building Materials departments posting positive comparable sales while other merchandising categories declined.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 33.4% from 33.8% primarily reflecting SRS inclusion partially offset by lower shrink in the Primary segment. SG&A expenses increased to 17.9% of sales from 17.6%, driven by higher payroll costs and deleverage from negative comparable sales, while depreciation and amortization rose to 2.0% from 1.8% due to SRS-related intangible amortization.
material_events: Completed $17.7 billion SRS acquisition on June 18, 2024, funded through commercial paper borrowings and long-term debt issuance. Increased quarterly dividend 7.7% to $2.25 per share in February 2024, paying $6.7 billion in dividends during nine months. Paused share repurchases in March 2024 ahead of SRS acquisition, completing $649 million in buybacks prior to suspension.
macro_exposures: Heightened macroeconomic uncertainties and persistent high interest rate environment continue pressuring home improvement demand, contributing to decreased comparable customer transactions and average ticket. Stronger U.S. dollar negatively impacted net sales by $131 million in Q3. Hurricane-related demand provided incremental sales benefit during the third quarter.
forward_outlook: Capital expenditure guidance maintained at $3.0-3.5 billion for fiscal 2024, approximately 2% of net sales, focused on new stores and customer experience improvements including technology investments. Management does not plan to resume share repurchases until excess cash is used to reduce outstanding debt following SRS acquisition financing.
uncertainty_profile: ROIC declined to 31.5% from 38.7% due to higher average debt and equity from SRS acquisition financing along with lower operating income. Outstanding commercial paper borrowings of $1.3 billion at quarter-end with weighted average rate of 4.8%, supported by revolving credit facilities. Business combination accounting for SRS remains subject to measurement period adjustments.
